mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength that lifts corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5, or quarterly forward returns are negative when both series grow concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns that force rapid equity repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are positive in >50% of months following an industrial production decline, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 5-year rolling window, or positive payroll surprises yield negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations trigger anticipated central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPI inflation exceeds 2.5 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a sector-specific reallocation of capital that reduces broad market liquidity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive when housing starts rise while retail sales decline, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary accommodation, rapid increases in industrial production reflect easing capacity constraints and improved profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative weekly returns in >55% of low-rate environments, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance during easing regimes.
